entries.title: Former US officials criticise Pentagon silence on deadly Iran school attack
entries.snippet: In the two months since the deadly strike, the US defence department has said only that the incident is under investigation.

entries.title: Five takeaways from the King's historic address to Congress
entries.snippet: There were some lines in the speech that may have buoyed Democrats – and raised eyebrows in the White House.

entries.title: Comey charged with threatening Trump's life in Instagram post
entries.snippet: The new case stems from a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director that the justice department says calls for violence against Trump.

entries.title: France urges citizens to leave Mali after rebel attacks
entries.snippet: The UK has issued similar advice, telling citizens who stay, that they do so at their own risk.

entries.title: Kim Jong Un praises troops who 'self-blasted' to avoid capture by Ukraine
entries.snippet: It confirms suspicions that North Korean soldiers are being told to detonate their grenades to kill themselves.

entries.title: Irish government announces further fuel supports after protests
entries.snippet: The Irish government has announced further fuel supports for a number of sectors after nationwide protests in recent weeks.

entries.title: Afghanistan women footballers can return to competition after FIFA rule changes
entries.snippet: Afghanistan women will become a "symbol of resilience" after Fifa approved their return to international competition, says former captain Khalida Popal.

entries.title: Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say
entries.snippet: They are among five people officials say were killed in successive attacks on Tuesday.

entries.title: Russian paramilitary carried out air strikes in Mali as rebels advanced, footage shows
entries.snippet: Last weekend's attacks came as the Africa Corps withdrew from a key base in northern Mali.

entries.title: Two dead after small plane crashes into Australia airport hangar
entries.snippet: The aircraft burst into flames after it crashed at Parafield Airport in Adelaide.

entries.title: Trump's face to feature on commemorative US passports
entries.snippet: The passports will be released as part of the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

entries.title: Tupac's family files wrongful death lawsuit in LA
entries.snippet: The suit seeks to uncover several "co-conspirators" who may have been involved in the star's death.

entries.title: Japan zoo staffer allegedly dumps wife's body inside incinerator
entries.snippet: The zoo has had to delay its reopening after a maintenance break, as police investigate the staff member's claim.

entries.title: Endangered antelopes flown to Kenya from Czech zoo in 'historic homecoming'
entries.snippet: The mountain bongo is a rare antelope endemic to Kenyan highland forests, with less than 100 in the wild.

entries.title: Outrage after Indian man carries his sister's skeleton to a bank to prove her death
entries.snippet: A video from Odisha state of Jitu Munda taking the remains to the bank went viral this week, leading to anger.

entries.title: US regulator to review Disney broadcast licences after Jimmy Kimmel joke about Melania Trump
entries.snippet: The move comes as the White House pressures Disney-owned ABC to fire Kimmel after he called Melania an "expectant widow".

entries.title: Musk accuses Altman of stealing a charity as courtroom battle begins
entries.snippet: The case over OpenAI's history and public commitments could have major implications for the future of AI.

entries.title: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
entries.snippet: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

entries.title: Path cleared for Everest climbers after huge ice block
entries.snippet: Experts warn there are still risks of further ice collapses, and there are fears climbers will again have to queue to reach the summit.

entries.title: Third Ukrainian strike hits Russian oil refinery and prompts evacuations
entries.snippet: Residents near a burning refinery in Tuapse are told to leave, and the Kremlin accuses Kyiv of destabilising global energy markets.

entries.title: SCOTUS weighs Temporary Protected Status cases. And, jury indicts James Comey again
entries.snippet: The Supreme Court is set to hear arguments on whether to end Temporary Protected Status for Haitians and Syrians. And, a grand jury has indicted former FBI Director James Comey for a second time.

entries.title: South Korean court sentences ex-President Yoon to 7 years in prison
entries.snippet: An appeals court sentenced ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest and bypassing a Cabinet meeting before his brief imposition of martial law in December 2024.

entries.title: Spirit Airlines tried to be the Dollar General of the skies. Then the big airlines beat it at its own game
entries.snippet: Spirit Airlines helped pioneer ultra-cheap flying and soared. Then legacy airlines copied them, outmaneuvered them with loyalty programs, and the economy turned against their core customers.

entries.title: Like soap operas, 75 feet up: How bald eagle nest cams hook online communities
entries.snippet: Across the country, some 50 bald eagle nests fitted with cameras broadcast up-close views of raptor family life. Every spring, as eggs hatch and eaglets grow, these cameras rake in millions of views.

entries.title: How a Supreme Court fight over fish oil could raise your prescription drug costs
entries.snippet: The justices are set to hear Hikma v. Amarin , a battle over drug patents that could raise costs for patients and change the way generic companies do business.

entries.title: How a Republican state lawmaker tried to let Holocaust deniers hijack history lessons
entries.snippet: A New Hampshire Republican. A German Holocaust denier. A suspicious bottle of baby oil. An NPR investigation reveals how the alarming rise of antisemitic conspiracy theories reached a state capitol.

entries.title: RFK Jr. talked about 'reparenting' kids on wellness farms. We visit one that inspired him
entries.snippet: U.S. Secretary of Health and Human Services Robert F. Kennedy Jr. says a farm community in Italy for people with addiction is a model for wellness camps designed to ease the U.S. overdose crisis. Critics say the idea is dangerous.

entries.title: Supreme Court weighs Trump's effort to end temporary protected status for Haitians, Syrians
entries.snippet: At issue is the TPS program, which permits eligible individuals to live and work in the United States if they cannot return to their home countries because of "extraordinary or temporary conditions."

entries.title: Could this conference be a 'turning point' for the world's use of fossil fuels?
entries.snippet: Against the backdrop of an energy crisis and a warming planet, more than 50 countries have come to Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss concrete ways to phase out oil, gas and coal.

entries.title: It's set to be Jerome Powell's last meeting as Fed chair -- as a big change looms
entries.snippet: The Fed is expected to hold rates steady, at what's likely to be Jerome Powell's last meeting as chair — with Kevin Warsh looking set to replace him.

entries.title: Senate Republicans reject attempt to end Trump's blockade of Cuba
entries.snippet: The vote on the war powers resolution showed how Republicans continue to stand behind Trump as he acts unilaterally to exert American force in a range of global conflicts, including Venezuela, Iran and Cuba.

entries.title: WATCH LIVE: Fed Chair Powell holds briefing on interest rate decision as his term nears end
entries.snippet: The Federal Reserve will meet this week ahead of a looming leadership transition that remains fuzzy, and Wednesday's news conference will be closely watched for any clarification.

entries.title: Actor Nathan Chasing Horse sentenced to life in prison for sexual assault
entries.snippet: A Nevada judge sentenced "Dances With Wolves" actor Nathan Chasing Horse on Monday to life in prison for sexually assaulting Indigenous women and girls. A jury had previously convicted him of 13 charges, mostly related to sexual assault of three women.

entries.title: James Comey indicted over social media post Trump's DOJ says crossed a line
entries.snippet: Former FBI director James Comey has once again been indicted by the Department of Justice. It's the second time the Trump administration has attempted to prosecute him. The latest is connected to a post Comey shared last year of seashells spelling out the numbers "86 47." The DOJ

entries.title: How the UAE's departure from OPEC could impact oil markets
entries.snippet: There seems to be little movement on the resumption of talks between the U.S. and Iran. There is also almost no movement through the Strait of Hormuz, the vital bottleneck that's essentially cut off 20% of the world's oil and gas supply. Tuesday, the UAE announced it will leave O

entries.title: Jack Thornell, Pulitzer-winning photographer of Civil Rights Movement, dies at 86
entries.snippet: Former Associated Press photographer Jack Thornell, whose Pulitzer Prize-winning picture of a shotgun-felled James Meredith looking back toward his would-be assassin on a Mississippi highway in 1966 became an enduring image of the Civil Rights Movement, has died.

entries.title: King Charles calls for unity in rare royal address to Congress
entries.snippet: The king and queen of England were received with high diplomatic fanfare at the White House on day two of the royal visit. King Charles became just the second monarch to address Congress. He delivered a message of unity and peace during a time of tense relations. Nick Schifrin re

entries.title: News Wrap: Federal agents search Minnesota childcare centers
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Tuesday, federal agents carried search warrants at childcare centers in Minnesota, a former advisor to Dr. Anthony Fauci at the National Institutes of Health has been indicted by a federal grand jury in Maryland for conspiring to hide records related to the pande

entries.title: FCC threatens ABC's licenses as Trumps call for Kimmel's firing
entries.snippet: The FCC is ramping up the pressure on ABC and Disney by threatening to strip broadcasters of their station licenses. The FCC says the review of the licenses is tied to Disney's DEI initiatives, but it's widely seen as retaliation in the ongoing battle between President Trump and

entries.title: The mental health toll and lifelong consequences of gun violence in America
entries.snippet: Gun violence continues to impact communities across the country daily. Many don't make the news, but create lifelong consequences for victims, their families and even entire neighborhoods. The normalization of shootings has created a persistent public health crisis. William Brang

entries.title: WATCH: Justice Department holds news briefing on Comey indictment over '86 47' photo
entries.snippet: Former FBI Director James Comey was indicted again Tuesday, this time in an investigation over a social media photo of seashells arranged on a beach that officials said constituted a threat against President Donald Trump.

entries.title: Families sound alarm on toxic mold in military housing
entries.snippet: Military families are sounding the alarm about the presence of toxic mold in military housing. It's an issue that has long been making service members and their families sick. Stephanie Sy reports on the ongoing problem and what is and isn't being done to address it.

entries.title: Tessa Thompson and Adrien Brody on Broadway debuts in 'The Fear of 13'
entries.snippet: In 2004, Nick Yarris walked out of a Pennsylvania prison after 22 years on death row. His was the first death row case in Pennsylvania overturned by DNA evidence. His wrongful conviction is now the focus of the new play "The Fear of 13." It stars Adrien Brody and Tessa Thompson i

entries.title: Students injured in Brown University shooting sue school over alleged security failures
entries.snippet: Three students who were injured in the December campus shooting at Brown University are each suing the Ivy League school, alleging it ignored prior warnings about the shooter and did not provide adequate security that could have prevented the tragedy.

entries.title: U.S. soldier pleads not guilty to using Maduro raid intel to win $400,000 on Polymarket
entries.snippet: A U.S. special forces soldier pleaded not guilty Tuesday to charges that he used classified information about the mission to capture former Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro to win more than $400,000 on the prediction market Polymarket.

entries.title: Starmer avoids ethics probe over Mandelson appointment but challenges lie ahead
entries.snippet: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Tuesday averted a parliamentary inquiry over his choice of Peter Mandelson as British ambassador to Washington, but failed to quell questions about whether he bent the rules to make the controversial appointment.

entries.title: James Comey indicted in probe over online post officials say constituted Trump threat
entries.snippet: The criminal case is the second in a matter of months against Comey and is part of the Trump administration Justice Department's relentless effort to prosecute political opponents of the Republican president.

entries.title: Taylor Swift files new trademark applications. One expert says it is to curb AI threats
entries.snippet: Swift has been a target of AI misuse in the past.

entries.title: Justice Department accuses former Fauci adviser of concealing communications related to COVID research
entries.snippet: A former senior adviser to Dr. Anthony Fauci has been indicted on federal charges alleging he conspired to hide his communications related to COVID-19 research as the pandemic unfolded.

entries.title: Ex-player Damon Jones first to plead guilty in basketball gambling sweep
entries.snippet: Former NBA player and assistant coach Damon Jones has become the first person to plead guilty in a gambling sweep that led to the arrests of more than 30 people.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Hungary inspires Israeli opposition bid to defeat Netanyahu
entries.snippet: Taking a cue from the ousting of Hungary's long-time leader, Viktor Orban, two Israeli opposition figures have joined forces to try to unseat Prime Minister Netanyahu.

entries.title: Iran war: UN rights chief 'appalled' by arrests, executions
entries.snippet: The UN rights office reports executions, mass arrests and a sweeping crackdown in Iran during the war, with thousands detained. Meanwhile, the country's currency has hit a record low. DW has the latest.

entries.title: UK: 2 people stabbed in north London, suspect arrested
entries.snippet: British police arrested a man after two people were stabbed in Golders Green, an area of north London with a large Jewish population. A Jewish community security organization said the wounded people were being treated.

entries.title: 'Rose' and the piece of fabric that embodies gender politics
entries.snippet: As the film starring Sandra Hüller is released in Germany, DW talked to director Markus Schleinzer about why women passing as men inspired his historical drama, and why it's a current topic.

entries.title: Why Germany wants a seat at the UN Security Council
entries.snippet: With the rules-based world order "under stress," Germany is on a mission to gain a coveted seat at the United Nation’s top table. German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul told DW why diplomacy still matters.

entries.title: Thailand ex-PM Thaksin Shinawatra is granted early parole
entries.snippet: The influential former prime minister of Thailand, Thaksin Shinawatra, is set to be released from prison in May. He was serving a one-year prison term for corruption that began in September.

entries.title: Why UAE's OPEC exit is a blow to Saudi Arabia
entries.snippet: The United Arab Emirates is leaving OPEC to pump more oil on its own terms. The break strips Saudi Arabia of a key partner and adds to growing uncertainty over the cartel's future.

entries.title: South Korea: Yoon's obstruction sentence hiked to 7 years
entries.snippet: Yoon has also been handed a life sentence for leading an insurrection in a separate trial. The prison sentences follow his failed attempt to impose martial law in 2024.

entries.title: Hungary's Magyar meets EU's von der Leyen in high-stakes Brussels visit
entries.snippet: Even before Peter Magyar takes office as Hungary's prime minister, he is looking to unlock €10 billion in EU funding ahead of an August deadline. Talks with Ursula von der Leyen come as part of a post-Orban EU reset.

entries.title: Israel in Lebanon: Is the 'security buffer zone' legal?
entries.snippet: Buffer zones occupy a grey area in international humanitarian law, as rules around them in contemporary warfare are not particularly clear.

entries.title: EU Parliament pushes for bigger long-term budget, new taxes
entries.snippet: The European Parliament has backed plans to boost the EU's long-term budget and introduce new taxes to support farming and poorer regions. The proposals have set up a clash with member states, including Germany.

entries.title: EU accuses Meta of allowing children under 13 on platforms
entries.snippet: The European Commission accused Meta, the company behind Instagram and Facebook, of breaching its own rules of barring children under the age of 13 from using its platforms.

entries.title: Germany news: Cabinet discusses 2027 budget plans as Iran war threatens finances
entries.snippet: The Cabinet hopes to sign off on core points of its 2027 budget, so it can start to move through parliament. Germany already plans massive borrowing next year, before adequately factoring in Iran-based costs. Follow DW.

entries.title: India news: High voter turnout in West Bengal elections despite violence
entries.snippet: Mamata Banerjee's TMC and Narendra Modi's BJP are locked in a high-stakes battle in the final round of voting. Meanwhile, a Dalit man talks about his experiences with the Indian diaspora in Germany. DW has the latest.

entries.title: Trump says King Charles agrees Iran cannot have nuclear bomb
entries.snippet: During a state dinner at the White House, US President Donald Trump said that Britain's King Charles III had agreed that Iran could not possess nuclear weapons. Trump also said that Iran had been "militarily defeated."

entries.title: Extreme weather and green energy on the rise in Europe
entries.snippet: Europe's climate extremes have hit new highs — but renewables are now supplying nearly half of the continent's electricity.

entries.title: US to produce passports featuring Trump's image
entries.snippet: Donald Trump's picture will soon appear in limited edition passports, commemorating the United States' 250th year of independence. It is the latest effort to slap Trump's face on US institutions and documents.

entries.title: Ex-FBI Director James Comey indicted again — official
entries.snippet: It is the second time the Justice Department under Donald Trump has indicted Comey. Comey is a frequent critic of the president.

entries.title: EU Parliament approves common definition of rape
entries.snippet: Until now, definitions of what constitutes rape under criminal law had varied significantly across the EU, ranging from "only yes means yes" to "only no means no."
